trigger.unexplained_reason: Bottle moved from robot to room without explicit causal evidence.

robot_description_capsule.summary: # Shadow Robot Self-Model ## Purpose This document defines the technical identity, components, capabilities, and limits of the Shadow robot. It is intended to serve as a grounded self-model for diagnosis, explanation, and hypothesis generation. ## Identity - Shadow is an indoor mobile social robot. - Shadow is designed for human-following, assistance, and operation in human-centered indoor environments. - Shadow is a ground robot. - Shadow is not an industrial manipulator. - Shadow is not a flying robot. - Shadow is not a stationary kiosk. - Shadow is not a telepresence-only platform. ## Core Grounding Rule - Valid reasoning about Shadow must only use components, subsystems, and constraints explicitly described in this document. - If a proposed fault depends on a component not listed here, that proposal is invalid. - Explanations must stay within the real hardware and software limits of Shadow. ## Physical Structure - Maximum width: 625 mm. - Maximum height: 2030 mm. - The chassis is modular and divided into 3 main printed sections. - The chassis is manufactured with FDM printing. - The structural material is TPU HARDNESS+. - The robot is designed to pass through standard indoor doorways. ## Locomotion and Mechanical System - Shadow uses a holonomic omnidirectional mobile base. - Shadow has 4 Mecanum wheels. - Each wheel is driven by an independent 150 W hub motor. - The 4 wheels are controlled through 2 dual-axis drivers. - The robot can move forward, backward, laterally, diagonally, and rotate in place. - The robot includes a micro-adjustable suspension system. - The suspension mechanically decouples the wheels from the main chassis. - The suspension includes steel rods, dampers, and springs. - The minimum operational speed is 3 km/h. ## Power and Electronics - Shadow is powered by a lithium battery. - Battery capacity: 1 kWh. - Nominal battery voltage: 48 V. - Maximum current draw: 22 A. - Expected autonomy: about 7 hours under normal conditions. - The battery is mounted low in the chassis to reduce the center of gravity. - The power electronics are organized in a modular extractable tray. - Defined power rails include 48 V, 24 V, 19 V, 12 V, and 5 V. - Shadow includes a 10 Gb/s Ethernet switch. - Shadow includes an external emergency stop button. ## Compute - Shadow has a single high-level compute platform: NVIDIA Jetson Orin. - Shadow has no redundant high-level compute platform. - Shadow has no secondary onboard AI brain described in this document. ## Internal Sensing - Shadow includes a WT901B AHRS-IMU. - The internal sensing layer monitors orientation and motion state. - Internal monitoring also includes voltmeters. - Internal monitoring also includes ammeters. - Internal monitoring also includes temperature sensors. - These sensors support state monitoring, stability supervision, and fault detection. ## External Perception - Shadow includes a Helios 3D LiDAR. - The Helios LiDAR supports forward mapping and obstacle detection. - Shadow includes a Bpearl 3D LiDAR. - The Bpearl LiDAR supports near-hemispherical volumetric perception around the robot. - Shadow includes a Ricoh Theta Z1 360 RGB camera. - The camera provides 360-degree visual perception using two fisheye views. - The primary visual use is human detection and semantic perception. - Shadow includes a front touch screen as human-machine interface. ## Software Architecture - Shadow uses a two-layer architecture. ### RoboComp Layer - RoboComp is the low-level layer.
selected_sentences: # Shadow Robot Self-Model ## Purpose This document defines the technical identity, components, capabilities, and limits of the Shadow robot.
It is intended to serve as a grounded self-model for diagnosis, explanation, and hypothesis generation.
## Identity - Shadow is an indoor mobile social robot.
- Shadow is designed for human-following, assistance, and operation in human-centered indoor environments.
- Shadow is a ground robot.
- Shadow is not an industrial manipulator.
- Shadow is not a flying robot.
- Shadow is not a stationary kiosk.
- Shadow is not a telepresence-only platform.
## Core Grounding Rule - Valid reasoning about Shadow must only use components, subsystems, and constraints explicitly described in this document.
- If a proposed fault depends on a component not listed here, that proposal is invalid.
- Explanations must stay within the real hardware and software limits of Shadow.
## Physical Structure - Maximum width: 625 mm.
- Maximum height: 2030 mm.
- The chassis is modular and divided into 3 main printed sections.
- The chassis is manufactured with FDM printing.
- The structural material is TPU HARDNESS+.
- The robot is designed to pass through standard indoor doorways.
## Locomotion and Mechanical System - Shadow uses a holonomic omnidirectional mobile base.
- Shadow has 4 Mecanum wheels.
- Each wheel is driven by an independent 150 W hub motor.
- The 4 wheels are controlled through 2 dual-axis drivers.
- The robot can move forward, backward, laterally, diagonally, and rotate in place.
- The robot includes a micro-adjustable suspension system.
- The suspension mechanically decouples the wheels from the main chassis.
- The suspension includes steel rods, dampers, and springs.
- The minimum operational speed is 3 km/h.
## Power and Electronics - Shadow is powered by a lithium battery.
- Battery capacity: 1 kWh.
- Nominal battery voltage: 48 V.
- Maximum current draw: 22 A.
- Expected autonomy: about 7 hours under normal conditions.
- The battery is mounted low in the chassis to reduce the center of gravity.
- The power electronics are organized in a modular extractable tray.
- Defined power rails include 48 V, 24 V, 19 V, 12 V, and 5 V.
- Shadow includes a 10 Gb/s Ethernet switch.
- Shadow includes an external emergency stop button.
## Compute - Shadow has a single high-level compute platform: NVIDIA Jetson Orin.
- Shadow has no redundant high-level compute platform.
- Shadow has no secondary onboard AI brain described in this document.
## Internal Sensing - Shadow includes a WT901B AHRS-IMU.
- The internal sensing layer monitors orientation and motion state.
- Internal monitoring also includes voltmeters.
- Internal monitoring also includes ammeters.
- Internal monitoring also includes temperature sensors.
- These sensors support state monitoring, stability supervision, and fault detection.
## External Perception - Shadow includes a Helios 3D LiDAR.
- The Helios LiDAR supports forward mapping and obstacle detection.
- Shadow includes a Bpearl 3D LiDAR.
- The Bpearl LiDAR supports near-hemispherical volumetric perception around the robot.
- Shadow includes a Ricoh Theta Z1 360 RGB camera.
- The camera provides 360-degree visual perception using two fisheye views.
- The primary visual use is human detection and semantic perception.
- Shadow includes a front touch screen as human-machine interface.
## Software Architecture - Shadow uses a two-layer architecture.
### RoboComp Layer - RoboComp is the low-level layer.

hypotheses.title: Undetected Floor Obstacle Collision
hypotheses.rationale: A low-profile obstacle like a cable or bump strip on the floor could cause the robot to jolt unexpectedly, dislodging the carried bottle. Such obstacles might not be detected by the LiDAR due to their low profile.
grounding: The Helios LiDAR supports forward mapping and obstacle detection
expected_observations: Robot chassis experiences sudden lateral force
Bottle is dislodged due to impact

hypotheses.title: External Force from Person Contact
hypotheses.rationale: A person in the environment could have accidentally bumped into the robot while it was carrying the bottle, causing the bottle to be dislodged. This would explain the bottle's movement without any robot fault.
grounding: Shadow is designed for human-following, assistance, and operation in human-centered indoor environments
expected_observations: Bottle is dislodged due to external impact

hypotheses.title: Slippery Floor Surface
hypotheses.rationale: A sudden change in floor friction could cause the robot's wheels to slip during motion, creating unexpected movement that dislodges the carried bottle. This could happen on a wet or polished surface.
grounding: Shadow uses a holonomic omnidirectional mobile base
expected_observations: Bottle becomes unstable due to unexpected motion

hypotheses.title: Wheel Drive Failure During Motion
hypotheses.rationale: A sudden failure in one of the Mecanum wheel motors or drivers could cause an unexpected motion that dislodges the carried bottle. The holonomic base relies on coordinated wheel control, and a single wheel failure can produce erratic movement.
grounding: Each wheel is driven by an independent 150 W hub motor
The 4 wheels are controlled through 2 dual-axis drivers
expected_observations: Robot exhibits lateral drift or rotation
Bottle loses stable placement during motion

hypotheses.title: Suspension System Malfunction
hypotheses.rationale: The micro-adjustable suspension system includes springs and dampers that decouple wheel motion from the chassis. A malfunction could cause sudden vertical or lateral movement that destabilizes carried objects.
grounding: The robot includes a micro-adjustable suspension system
The suspension mechanically decouples the wheels from the main chassis
The suspension includes steel rods, dampers, and springs
expected_observations: Carried bottle becomes unstable due to chassis movement

hypotheses.title: IMU Orientation Sensor Drift
hypotheses.rationale: If the WT901B AHRS-IMU experiences drift or failure, the robot's perception of its own orientation could be incorrect, leading to unstable motion that dislodges the carried bottle.
grounding: The internal sensing layer monitors orientation and motion state
hypotheses.untestable_reason: Hypothesis declared untestable by the generator (no catalog intervention applies). Generator note: IMU drift simulation requires complex sensor model modifications not available in current intervention catalog
expected_observations: Robot moves with incorrect orientation perception
Bottle placement becomes unstable due to incorrect motion
